detaileddescription.para: User number or the controller index of the player
Credentials of the account that we wish to login with
String representing the error code that occurred
Object representing the error code that occurred
Digit code representing the error that occurred
Type of login that we wish to do on the backend
Online user account for the user that we were able to login as
Login status for the user, should be NotLoggedIn, unless login succeeds fully
Flag if login by External UI is failed. It will retry the Native login and skip the login UI

memberdef.argsstring: (FOnlineSubsystemAccelByte *const InABSubsystem, int32 InLocalUserNum, const FOnlineAccountCredentials &InAccountCredentials, bool bInCreateHeadlessAccount=true)
memberdef.name: FOnlineAsyncTaskAccelByteLogin

detaileddescription.para: Basic initialize override to check if we are using timeouts, and if so update the last task update time to the current time.
Basic method to get the current name of the task, used for ToString on tasks as well as trace logs.
Should be overridden by any async task that extends from this base class.
Attempts to fire off a login request with a native subsystem, if one is set up and usable.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the user that we want to try and auth with native subsystem pass through

simplesect.para: bool that is true if a native subsystem login was fired off, or false if not

memberdef.argsstring: (int32 NativeLocalUserNum, bool bWasNativeLoginSuccessful, const FUniqueNetId &NativeUserId, const FString &NativeError)
memberdef.name: OnNativeLoginComplete

detaileddescription.para: Callback for delegate fired when the native subsystem has finished its authentication. Authenticates with the AccelByte back end upon firing.

memberdef.argsstring: (TSharedPtr< const FUniqueNetId > UniqueId, const int ControllerIndex, const FOnlineError &NativeError)
memberdef.name: OnNativeLoginUIClosed

detaileddescription.para: Callback for when the login UI for a native platform subsystem is closed. Used to allow sign in with local user accounts on a native subsystem and then subsequently login with the AccelByte back end. Passing in an extra LocalUserNum, as ControllerIndex is -1 if the call fails and we need to be able to inform that the login failed.
Perform login on the AccelByte backend using defined login type and OAuth error type
Delegate handler for when any AccelByte login call succeeds.
Delegate handler for when any AccelByte login call fails.

parameterdescription.para: Code returned from the backend representing the error that was encountered with the request
Message returned from the backend describing the error that was encountered with the request

detaileddescription.para: Delegate handler for when any AccelByte login call fails.

parameterdescription.para: Code returned from the backend representing the error that was encountered with the request
Message returned from the backend describing the error that was encountered with the request
Object representing the error code that occurred

detaileddescription.para: Async task to authenticate a user with the AccelByte backend, either using a native platform account, or a user specified account
